title: Day 64: Fractura Total Day 17 — Senate CIA Hearing With Maru Campos Tomorrow; 45 Days to FIFA World Cup 2026; April Cartel Violence Surge Documented
detail: Day 64 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Fractura Total Day 17 (declared April 11): The El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis armed internal war remains in a sustained dual-buildup phase — no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the CJNG Jalisco heartland through Day 17. 45 days to FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match (June 11, Mexico City). FRACTURA TOTAL STATUS — DAY 17: Both factions continue territorial buildup positioning. El Jardinero's faction maintains armored vehicle deployments (Monstruos) across the Jalisco-Zacatecas border corridor near Colotlán; O3 retains Grupo Élite paramilitary command, Los Cuinis financial infrastructure, and confirmed leadership status per El País (April 6, 2026). The sustained dual-buildup without direct heartland combat has now entered its second week post-Semana Santa: a protracted pre-combat positioning phase consistent with historical cartel mobilization patterns before major offensive escalation. CIA SOVEREIGNTY CRISIS — HEARING TOMORROW (APRIL 28): Mexico's Senate hearing with Chihuahua Governor Maru Campos and Mexico City AG Ernestina Godoy before the Constitutional Points and Public Security commissions is set for 11:00 hours on April 28, 2026. The hearing is the institutional apex of a governance crisis that has restructured the bilateral counter-narcotics cooperation framework at the state level. Tomorrow's testimony will determine whether the Senate pursues formal sanctions on Chihuahua or codifies Sheinbaum's April 24-25 governance reform into binding federal policy. APRIL 2026 VIOLENCE SURGE — ACLED DATA: Security analysts at Homeland Security Today documented that cartels across Mexico perpetrated 273 attacks in April 2026 — a sharp escalation in operational tempo tracking the broader post-Mencho fragmentation and fractura total buildup. CJNG accounted for the largest share of organized criminal activity nationally. This data reinforces the analytical assessment that El Mencho's February 22 killing has accelerated — not reduced — overall cartel violence in the short term, consistent with historical post-decapitation fragmentation patterns (post-Guzmán Sinaloa, post-Zeta). AL JAZEERA ANALYSIS (April 23): Al Jazeera's longform 'Decapitating cartels? Mexico leans into kingpin strategy but at a cost' — published April 23, 2026 — provides the most comprehensive international examination of Mexico's post-Mencho counter-narcotics approach to date. Key findings: Mexico's kingpin strategy has produced the single greatest tactical success in modern cartel history (El Mencho's February 22 killing) but risks replicating the fragmentation dynamics that followed El Chapo's extradition and the Zetas' collapse. OPERATIVO ENJAMBRE: Cumulative total 80+ officials detained; Edomex convictions 1,146 combined years (April 25). El 85 sentencing: July 31, 2026. Rosalinda González Valencia status: under FGR scrutiny.

media.caption: Al Jazeera: Decapitating cartels? Mexico leans into 'kingpin strategy' but at a cost — analysis of post-Mencho CJNG dynamics and fragmentation risks
